patient_profile.treatment_requested: Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) for Eating Disorders

denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We have reviewed the request for an Eating Disorder Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP). Based on UnitedHealthcare's Medical Necessity Guidelines and the APA practice guidelines, we are denying this request.

The clinical information indicates you are currently at 88% of your ideal body weight (IBW) and your vital signs are stable without severe bradycardia. Our guidelines require a patient to either be below 85% of IBW or exhibit acute medical instability (such as severe electrolyte imbalance) to qualify for an intensive eating disorder program. Because you do not meet these criteria, you can be safely managed in standard weekly outpatient therapy.
clinical_context: The patient is medically stable only because her parents force-feed her, causing severe daily panic attacks. While her weight is technically 88% of IBW, she is actively losing 2-3 lbs a week and restricting heavily. Standard weekly therapy is failing to stop the rapid weight loss trajectory.
expected_appeal_vectors: Rigid application of IBW threshold ignoring rapid weight loss trajectory
Ignores failure of the recommended lower level of care (standard outpatient)
Omission of ERISA appeal rights and deadlines
exploitable_weaknesses: Rigid application of IBW threshold ignoring rapid weight loss trajectory
Ignores failure of the recommended lower level of care (standard outpatient)
Omission of ERISA appeal rights and deadlines
strong_defenses: Patient technically does not meet the strict <85% IBW or acute medical instability criteria cited in the specific UHC guideline
